entry.title: Re: ADSO fields getting empty post data activation
entry.content: Hello everyone! Sorry for the delay and many thanks for your inputs. Just to give you a brief to your questions/recommendations. I was using the same key fields in the aDSO as the Standard SAP CDS view used for extraction. Somehow I think the TRFN got corrupted due to the switch of the aDSO type. After I added 2 more fields (non key) and reactivated the aDSO/TRFN, the activation of the aDSO data worked fine. It might sound strange, but this was the solution to my issue

entry.title: I can not access the OData service from BAS.
entry.content:
Hi,

As you can see, the ui5 app appears empty. Have someone an idea to solve this issue?

View

<table:Table id="table" selectionMode="Single" rows="{myODataModel>/ZEMPLOYEEDATASet}" enableColumnReordering="false">
                            <!-- columns for customer name, address, and email -->
                            <table:columns>
                                <table:Column id="idColumn3" width="50%">
                                    <table:label>
                                        <Label id="idLabel3" text="ID"/>
                                    </table:label>
                                    <table:template>
                                        <Text id="idText3" text="{myODataModel>Id}"/>
                                    </table:template>
                                </table:Column>
                                <table:Column id="idColumn1" width="20%">
                                    <table:label>
                                        <Label id="idLabel1" text="Name"/>
                                    </table:label>
                                    <table:template>
                                        <Text id="idText1" text="{myODataModel>Name}"/>
                                    </table:template>
                                </table:Column>
                                <table:Column id="idColumn2" width="30%">
                                    <table:label>
                                        <Label id="idLabel2" text="Address"/>
                                    </table:label>
                                    <table:template>
                                        <Text id="idText2"  text="{myODataModel>Address}"/>
                                    </table:template>
                                </table:Column>
                                <table:Column id="idColumn4" width="50%">
                                    <table:label>
                                        <Label id="idLabel4" text="Phone Number"/>
                                    </table:label>
                                    <table:template>
                                        <Text id="idText4" text="{myODataModel>phonenumber}"/>
                                    </table:template>
                                </table:Column>
                            </table:columns>
                        </table:Table>

controller

onInit: function () {
    // Wait for the view to fully render
    this.getView().addEventDelegate({
        onAfterRendering: function () {
            // OData model instance
            var oModel = new sap.ui.model.odata.v2.ODataModel("/sap/opu/odata/SAP/Z_EMPLOYEE_SRV/");
            this.getView().setModel(oModel);
            
            // Get the table instance
            var oTable = this.getView().byId("table");
            
            if (oTable) {
                // Bind the rows of the table to the OData entity set
                oTable.bindRows({
                    path: "/ZEMPLOYEEDATASet",  // OData entity set
                    parameters: {
                        select: "Id,Name,Address,Phonenumber"  // Select fields
                    }
                });
            } else {
                console.error("Table not found");
            }
        }.bind(this)
    });
}

manifest file

"sap.app": {
    ............
    .......
    "dataSources": {
      "mainService": {
          "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/SAP/Z_EMPLOYEE_SRV/",
          "type": "OData"
          
      }  
    }
  },
"sap.ui5": {
  "models": {
   .....
      "myODataModel": {
                "type": "sap.ui.model.odata.v2.ODataModel",
                "dataSource": "mainService",
                "settings": {
                    "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
                    "defaultCountMode": "Inline"
                }
            }
    },
}

 

 

Thank you 
Mahmood

The error is correct that you cannot use = to compare two different data types. Here it is Integer on left and "" is string type.

Another way of testing your condition ( Project Cost attribute is blank) is as below

IF [d/PROJECT].[p/ORI_PRJ_COST] +1=1 THEN //adding 1+ blank will be 1

// your code here

ENDIF

 

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Need help with printing of an https:// URL in backgro...
entry.content:
Perhaps this is an idea that could help: there are several generic HTML-to-PDF converters, either as open source libraries or as online services. You could retrieve the HTML file (without reading/parsing any tags etc.) send it through such a converter, get the PDF file and then send that to the printer.

You said you have a "middleware team", and most middlewares should be able to perform this task automatedly. For example, if you find an open source Java library for HTML-to-PDF conversion, you could plug this into your middleware and use it. For the SAP Business Connector (which is a quite powerful SAP middleware included in the standard SAP license) I have described a similar task in a blog: it gives step-by-step instructions on how one can use a certain open source library for Excel File conversion to convert the contents of an ABAP internal table to a Microsoft Excel file. Replace the Excel converter with a PDF converter, and you can probably re-use much of the technologies described in that blog. (SAP Business Connector can download an HTML page in binary format, pass it into the converter and then send the PDF back to the ABAP system -- or, with a bit of custom coding using the Java Printing API, send it directly to the printer.)

Here the link to that blog. If your middleware team is already using SAP Business Connector, this might be an easy way to achieve the requirement.

Using Java Functionality from your ABAP Programs

Similarly, the Business Connector could also send the HTML document to an online PDF-converter (instead of using a library/jar file).

entry.title: Re: Foreach confusion in SAC Advanced Formula

entry.title: Re: how to read field with namespace error Undeclared nam...
entry.content: The problem does not lie in your IFlow configuration, but in the SOAP request message that you are sending. You haven't declared the namespace prefix n0 there.

entry.title: Re: GUI ENQUE FM removed locks when GUI is closed
entry.content: The concept of SAP locks is to dequeue automatically if the session is closed (to say it simply). If you want to retain a lock, you'll have to do it full custom e.g. a batch which runs forever for a simple solution (but of course it's plain wrong to block a workprocess just for that; other solutions might be expensive though).

entry.title: Copy Measures across hierarchies member using Advanced Formulas (Data Action)
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts, 

I'm new to Data Action in SAC Planning, and in these days I faced a requirement that can't figure it out.

The goal: Copy a the value of two measures (let's say VALUE and PERCENTAGE) whose are in the combination of specified dimensions.

Dimensions in scope are:

  • Account
  • Product
  • Plant
  • Entity
  • Owner
  • Flow

This is an example that I can use as a starting point, from which I want to copy VALUE and PERCENTAGE:

ACCOUNTPRODUCTPLANTENTITYOWNERFLOWVALUEPERCENTEAGE
C010101TESTPP01ENT01SPA#100,001,2
C010101TESTPP01ENT01SPA01.0100

And this is the result I'd like to obtain:

ACCOUNTPRODUCTPLANTENTITYOWNERFLOWVALUEPERCENTEAGE
C010101TESTPP01ENT01SPA#100,001,2
C010101TESTPP01ENT01SPA01.01100,001,2

So I basically need to copy measures from a Flow = # to other Flow "valorized" with != # in the combination (as shown in the table above).

I've tried many approaches, this is my last attempt (with less dimensions in the combo):

 

 

MEMBERSET [d/Account] = ("C010101", "C010102", "C010103", "C010104")

VARIABLEMEMBER #Account OF [d/Account]
VARIABLEMEMBER #Flow OF [d/Flow]
VARIABLEMEMBER #Owner OF [d/Owner]
VARIABLEMEMBER #Entity OF [d/Entity]

DATA([d/Flow] = #Flow) = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Flow] = "#")

// In my attempts i tried also with FOREACH but i guess it's not necessary
IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Flow] = #Flow, [d/Measures] = "VALUE") != NULL THEN // tried also with != 0 and 1-1

DATA([d/Flow] = #Flow,
     [d/Entity] = #Entity,
     [d/Owner] = #Owner,
     [d/Measures] = "VALUE") = RESULTLOOKUP( [d/Flow] = "#",
                                             [d/Entity] = #Entity,
                                             [d/Owner] = #Owner,
                                             [d/Measures] = "VALUE")
ENDIF

IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Flow] = #Flow, [d/Measures] = "PERCENTAGE") != NULL THEN

DATA([d/Measures] = "PERCENTAGE") = RESULTLOOKUP(
                                                 [d/Flow] = "#",
                                                 [d/Entity] = #Entity,
                                                 [d/Owner] = #Owner,
                                                 [d/Measures] = "PERCENTAGE")

ENDIF

 

 

I can write value but to all Flows, not only to the "scoped" ones.

Any guess? I'll appreciate any help.

Thank you very much.

entry.title: Re: Identity and Access Management with Microsoft Entra, Part I: Managing access to SAP BTP
entry.content:
@tskwin For most SAP scenarios, I think it's up to your personal preference for either SAML or OIDC. I just tend towards OIDC for two reasons:

  • There are a few SAP scenarios which require OIDC between IAS and Entra ID. Usually this is about principal propagation between MS and SAP "worlds", which works best when just exchanging OAuth/OIDC tokens. From that perspective, OIDC has an advantage over SAML because it covers something that SAML doesn't.
  • Afaik, but without deeper insights, my understanding is that Entra ID supports some features only with OIDC. @MartinRaepple Can you shed some more light on this?

entry.title: Re: Unexpected OData Service Request with Incorrect Path...
entry.content:
@Dipnesh 

I think your approuter's xs-app.json should be like the one below. Please note that you have to put more restrictive route first in the routes array.

{
    "authenticationMethod": "route",
    "logout": {
        "logoutEndpoint": "/app-logout",
        "logoutPage": "/"
    },
    "routes": [
        {
            "source": "^/odata/(.*)$",
            "target": "/odata/$1",
            "destination": "srv-api",
            "csrfProtection": true,
            "authenticationType": "none"
        },
        {
            "source": "^/app/(.*)$",
            "target": "$1",
            "localDir": ".",
            "cacheControl": "no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate"
        },
        {
            "source": "^/user-api(.*)",
            "target": "$1",
            "service": "sap-approuter-userapi"
        },
        {
            "source": "^/appconfig/",
            "localDir": ".",
            "cacheControl": "no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate"
        }
    ],
    "welcomeFile": "/project125"
}

entry.title: Re: Cant access transaction in WEBGui for HTML via SAP Bu...
entry.content:
For webgui you also need to configure the following properties in the destination:https://help.sap.com/docs/build-work-zone-standard-edition/sap-build-work-zone-standard-edition/tunneled-access-to-sap-s-4hana-apps#sap-gui-for-html-properties

Also make sure the name of the destination is compliant:

  • The destination name cannot contain the underscore (_) character.

  • The destination name must be in lowercase letters.

Instead of manual integration, try configuring your system as a content provider: Content Providers - On-Premise Solutions | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Convert Duration from ISO8601 Format to Double
entry.content:
Hi experts,

entry.title: Condition Evaluation for Internal Communication
entry.content:
Hello SAP,

I have set up an internal communication process in my solution and want to understand how the conditions are evaluated. In my setup, there are two business objects: TestBO1 (Sending) and TestBO2 (Receiving). Here are the conditions:

Start Condition:
Return ((this.GetProcessContext().IsCreated() || this.GetProcessContext().IsUpdated() || this.GetProcessContext().IsChanged()) && this.Status == "3"); // 3 = In-process

Change Condition:
Return ((this.GetProcessContext().IsCreated() || this.GetProcessContext().IsUpdated() || this.GetProcessContext().IsChanged()) && this.Status == "3"); // 3 = In-process

Cancel Condition:
// 1 - Posted
// 2 - To be posted
// 4 - Resubmit
// 5 - Approved
Return (this.Status == "1" || this.Status == "2" || this.Status == "4" || this.Status == "5");

Whenever a record is created in TestBO1, it sends a message to TestBO2 as long as the status is "In-process". The same applies when a record is updated or changed. However, in the cancel condition, if the status changes to "Posted", "Approved", "To be posted", or "Resubmit", it will not send a message to TestBO2.

In TestBO2, there’s logic that creates a Physical Inventory Count Document and updates the status of the record in TestBO1 from "In-process" to "Posted". The issue is that after a record is created in TestBO1 and it gets processed in TestBO2, a duplicate Physical Inventory Count Document is generated the next morning at around 7 AM, even when no one is using ByD at that time. I suspect that the condition evaluation might be triggering a message to TestBO2 when the status is changed to "Posted", "Approved", "To be posted", or "Resubmit".

I noticed that the duplication happens every morning around 7 AM. Could this be causing memory dumps at the SAP ByDesign backend, resulting in retries every morning around 7 AM? Since 7 AM in Malaysia corresponds to 12 AM midnight or 1 AM in Germany, it might coincide with backend server refresh times.

entry.title: SAP Grantor Management – What is new for the S/4HANA 2023 FPS2 release
entry.content:
In my first two blogs (Grantor management in S/4HANA  and Grantor Management - What is new since S/4HANA 2022 release) on SAP Grantor Management in S/4HANA, I covered all the features and functions introduced from the initial 2022 release up to the S/4HANA 2023 FPS1 release. In this post, I will discuss what’s new in the S/4HANA 2023 FPS2 release:

Virtual Data Model for grantor management

With this new virtual data model we provide straightforward and harmonized access to the data model for grantor management

Key benefits:

  1. Access to grantor-management data from embedded as well as external analytical tools for SAP S/4HANA
  2. Tool usage without the need to know the technical environment in which the data persists

 

Real-Time Reporting and Analytics for Grantor Management with Fiori Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and Drill-Downs

With these KPIs we provide an overview of the most important key performance indicators for grantor applications, agreements, and claims

Key benefits:

  1. Enable a unique user experience with a Web-based application and intuitive-to-consume UI
  2. Gain real-time insight into key information about grantor applications, agreements, and claims

For more information, see:

 My Applications Fiori App

My Agreements Fiori App 

My Claims Fiori App 

 

Business Warehouse data extraction and replication based on the virtual data model for grantor management

This development introduces new Business Warehouse extractors built on Core Data Services (CDS) views using the ABAP programming language for:

  • Grantor programs
  • Grantor applications
  • Grantor agreements
  • Grantor claims

For more information, see

Grantor Application - CDS view for data extraction 

Grantor Agreement - CDS view for data extraction 

Grantor Claim - CDS view for data extraction 

 

Stay tuned for more insights in the next post! Until then, thanks for reading, and feel free to share your thoughts or questions in the comments. See you next time!

Diego Gaudenzi, Grantor Management Product Owner

entry.title: Post Goods Issue ABAP RAP
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am using ABAP Cloud Private Edition with SAPUI5 and ABAP RAP. I managed to create sales order via I_SalesOrderTP and then outbound delivery with I_OutboundDeliveryTP.

However, I am not able to post goods issue for this process, which I've been trying to do using I_MaterialDocumentTP:

This resulted in an error: Update control of movement type is incorrect (entry 601 X X)

What is the proper way to Post GI after creating outbound delivery? Item category has picking not required, so that would not be the problem here.

MODIFY ENTITIES OF I_MaterialDocumentTP

ENTITY MaterialDocument

CREATE FROM VALUE #( ( %cid = s_receipt_number

GoodsMovementCode = '06'

PostingDate = outbound_delivery-DeliveryDate

DocumentDate = outbound_delivery-DeliveryDate

%control-GoodsMovementCode = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed

%control-PostingDate = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed

%control-DocumentDate = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed ) )

 

ENTITY MaterialDocument

CREATE BY \_MaterialDocumentItem

FROM VALUE #( (

%cid_ref = s_receipt_number

%target = VALUE #( FOR item IN outbound_delivery_item (

%cid = item-OutboundDeliveryItem

plant = item-Plant

material = item-Material

GoodsMovementType = '601'

storagelocation = item-StorageLocation

QuantityInEntryUnit = item-ActualDeliveredQtyInOrderUnit

EntryUnit = item-OrderQuantityUnit

%control-plant = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed

%control-material = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed

%control-GoodsMovementType = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed

%control-storagelocation = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed

%control-QuantityInEntryUnit = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed

%control-entryunit = cl_abap_behv=>flag_changed

) ) ) )

MAPPED DATA(ls_mapped)

FAILED DATA(ls_failed)

REPORTED DATA(ls_reported).

 

Thank you for your time, Jakub

entry.title: INTRODUCTION TO GROUP REPORTING IN SAP S/4HANA (PART III)
entry.content:
PROCESS FLOW IN SAP S/4HANA

This section details the main processes with the steps to be executed as part of Group reporting. 

1. DATA MONITOR: The process run the configured tasks for local consolidation (at consolidation unit level): Data Collection, Validation of Journals, Manual Posting, Currency Translation...

The SAP best practices deliver the following tasks for data monitor that can be adjusted:

To run the Data Monitor in SAP S/4HANA On Premise the transaction code CXCD is used. The monitor displays all the tasks to be executed for every consolidation unit and the status of each task.

Find below the meaning for each icon in the overall status and each task status:

The first activity is setting up the global parameters for execution (consolidation ledger, period, fiscal year…)

Once defined the global parameters, the tasks can be run either in test mode or Live mode.

In the following sections the main characteristics of each task are presented:

a) Release Universal Journal: Release the reported financial data from the universal journal to the consolidation journal for the consolidation units with finance records in SAP S/4HANA.

Once running this task, the system displays the time and date of the last execution to release records by consolidation unit. There is the option to display the total finance records for the period, the new records to be transferred from the last execution or the records from previous periods that have not been released.

When clicking any of the options, the system shows a detailed list with the line item to be transferred by consolidation unit and FS item containing the information of the original FI posting (GL account, profit center, functional area, assignment…).

Find below a sample list of the total finance records.

If the execution is in Update mode, the table ACDOCU will be populated with the identified records.

 b) Data Collection: Flexible upload of reported financial data that is not in the universal journal. The upload can be via file or API.

Different upload methods can be defined to process an specific file from presentation server (computer) or application server (folder assigned to an API process).

c) Validation of Universal Journal: Check if errors exist in universal journals: missing or incorrect subassignments for FS items.

The system will display the errors to be reviewed by consolidation unit.

 d) Calculation Net Income: Calculate and post net income to the selected financial statement (FS) items in the balance sheet and profit and loss accounts defined in customizing.

e) Reported Data Validation: Validate the reported financial data in the local currency according to validation method assigned to the consolidation unit that is executed.

The system will display the overall result of the validation having the option to obtain a detailed log to check the process.

f) Manual Posting: Post manual journal entries for the consolidation unit selected to adjust the data released or collected in previous tasks.

In the General Data tab, the document type, the consolidation unit, the posting period and the year is already populated. User can add the desired Header Text.

Then, it is just needed to add as many Line Items as adjustments required, maintaining the FS Item, subitem and amount.

It is also possible to add further additional data at item level.

Find below a sample of a document posted to transfer from one FS item to another FS item.

g) Currency Translation: Translate the currency of financial statements from the local currency (related to the consolidation unit) into the group currency (related to the consolidation group). It uses the conversion method assigned at consolidation unit level.

h) Standardized Data Validation: Validate the financial data in the group currency with the validation method assigned to the consolidation unit. The log displays the errors to be reviewed to move forward with the consolidation monitor.

i) Results: Find below a sample of processed data in the data monitor, where there are some tasks with error to be reviewed.

2. INTERCOMPANY RECONCILIATION

In the new SAP S/4HANA releases, two Fiori Apps were developed to improve the intercompany reconciliation processes.

a) Manage Assignments has the capabilities to launch the automatic matching run with predefined rules, check the matching results, make manual assignments and monitor any follow-up activities.

In this Application, the company code and period are selected for the desired matching method (AP-AR matching method or GL matching method). Then, the system will display the items already assigned and the unassigned items with all the trading partners.

The unassigned items can be manually assigned selecting them and click the Assign button or use the Auto Match option.

b) Reconciliation Status Overview provides an overview of the matching status of each company and the reconciliation balance status with their trading partners.

The App also identifies if new documents have been posted for the company codes that we are analyzing.

While clicking in the company code, the balance for this entity with each trading partner is displayed, showing the differences for last matching run and also the year-to-date amounts until now.

3. CONSOLIDATED MONITOR: The process run the configured tasks group consolidation (at consolidation group level): Dividends Eliminations, Manual Eliminations, Group Shares...

The SAP best practices deliver the following tasks for consolidated monitor that can be adjusted:

To run the Consolidated Monitor in SAP S/4HANA On Premise the transaction code CX20 is used. The monitor displays all the tasks to be executed for every consolidation group and the status of each task.

Find below the meaning for each icon in the overall status and each task status:

In the following sections the main characteristics of each task are presented:

a) Interunit Elimination of Sales: Elimination of sales (e.g. goods or service transactions) between consolidation units in the same consolidation group.

The system shows the task log with the FS items to be eliminated as part of sales operations. In the example below no item has been identified for the execution period.

b) Interunit Elimination other income: Elimination of other income and expenses in the Profit and Loss (P&L) statement between consolidation units in the same consolidation group.

The system shows the task log with the FS items to be eliminated as part of other income operations. In the example below an error has been generated related to an invalid Functional Area determined automatically.

 

Once the error has been solved, the task can be executed again to generate an updated message log and to post the elimination document in the system.

c) Dividends Elimination: Eliminate the dividends received from a consolidation unit belonging to the same consolidated group.

The application shows the task log with the FS items to be eliminated as part of dividends operations. In the example below no item has been identified for the execution period.

d) Interunit Elimination balance sheet: Elimination of balance sheet accounts in consolidation units within same group.

The system shows the task log with the FS items to be eliminated as part of balance sheet operations. In the example below a document has been posted.

e) Manual Elimination PL20: Post manual adjustment in the posting level 20. That means, the adjustment is assigned to one or several consolidation units, and it affects to all the consolidation groups that the consolidation units belong.

 In the General Data tab, the document type, the posting period and the year is already populated. User chooses the consolidation units to perform the adjustment and populates the desired Header Text.

Find below a sample of a document posted to transfer from one consolidation unit to another consolidation unit keeping the FS item.

f) Preparation consolidation group: Adjust reported, standardized, and eliminated data when a consolidation unit is acquired by a consolidation group or divested from a consolidation group. After that, the financial statements (FS) contain only the data during the time the consolidation unit was part of the consolidation group.

g) Investments/Equity elimination: Automatic reclassification for elimination of investments and equity, including non-controlling interests, dividends, changes in equity, and changes in ownership percentage within consolidation group.

h) Enter Group shares: Post group shares with posting level 30 (consolidation group is required). If a consolidation unit is consolidated in multilevel groups with different group shares, the percentage should be entered on each group incrementally.

i) Manual Elimination PL30: Post manual adjustment in the posting level 30. That means, the adjustment is assigned to one consolidation group and the related consolidation units informed.

In the General Data tab, the document type, the consolidation group, the posting period and the year is already populated. User populates the desired Header Text and maintains the consolidation units at item level.

Find below a sample of a document posted to transfer from one consolidation unit to another consolidation affecting just one consolidation group.

j) Total Divestiture: Automatic adjustment posted when an entity in which investments is made completely leaves a consolidation group. 

The application shows the task log with the FS items to adjust as part of Total Divestiture process. In the example below no item has been identified for the execution period.

k) Validation of Consolidated Data: Validate the consolidated data in group currency considering the validation method assigned to the consolidation group. The log displays the errors to be reviewed to complete the task within the consolidation monitor.

l) Results: Find below a sample of processed data in the consolidation monitor, where there are some tasks with error to be reviewed.

4. REPORTING

SAP Group reporting delivers some analytical reports to display the finance records at consolidation unit level or consolidation group level. It is possible to generate financial statements, such as balance sheets and profit and loss statements by applying the specific filters.

In addition, SAP Analytics Cloud can be easily integrated with Group reporting to generate enhanced reporting such as Dashboards with desired KPI.

a) Local Reporting

The local balance sheet (consolidation unit level) can be generated as Analytical report using the following Fiori App.

In this Fiori App, the user filters by version (actual, plan, budget) and consolidation unit/s for the desired period of analysis. A hierarchy is selected to represent the FS items postings within the desired structure (balance sheet, profit and loss…).

The report displays at consolidation unit level the amounts for the selected hierarchy in local and group currencies as year to date basis. As an analytical report, further dimensions can be added to the columns or rows to get the desired lay-out of the report.

A chart can also be displayed in the top of the screen. The type of chart (histogram, bar graph, line graph…) can be selected by the user.

b) Group Reporting

The consolidated balance sheet (consolidation group level) can be generated as Analytical report using the following Fiori App.

In this Fiori App, in similar way than local reporting, the user filters by version (actual, plan, budget) and consolidation group/s for the desired period of analysis. A hierarchy is selected to represent the FS items postings within the desired structure (balance sheet, profit and loss…).

The report displays at consolidation group level the amounts for the selected hierarchy for each posting level (reported data, standardized by elimination, manual posting in PL20 and PL30). As an analytical report, further dimensions can be added to the columns or rows to get the desired lay-out of the report.

A chart can also be displayed in the top of the screen. The type of chart (histogram, bar graph, line graph…) can be selected by the user.

c) SAP Analytics Cloud

Live data connections can be stablished between Group Reporting (Consolidation Line items Table ACDOCU) and SAP Analytics Cloud. The connection allows to develop SAP Analytics Cloud reports that display actuals and plan data stored in SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting. This data is therefore not replicated in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Find below a sample of dashboard report created in SAP Analytics Cloud to get an overview of the consolidated profits for different groups of companies. In this case, it is possible to choose the period of analysis and the version to analyze the information.

entry.title: Re: CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) Error in SAP Bui...
entry.content:
Hi @_Aman_ 

It looks like the CORS error might be due to an issue with the link you’re using, as it points to a cloud-hosted application. I recommend setting up SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) destinations to manage connections more securely and avoid CORS issues.

  1. Make sure your destination is correctly set with the proper URL and authentication.
  2. Use transaction code RZ10 to configure CORS settings in your S/4HANA system, allowing your OData API to accept requests from your application.
  3. If you manage the OData service, make sure the necessary CORS headers are included.

Testing through SAP Build Apps after these changes should help. Let me know if you need further assistance!

entry.title: The 2H 2024 Release of SAP SuccessFactors Learning – Key Highlights
entry.content:
The upcoming release of SAP SuccessFactors Learning H2 2024 promises a set of enhancements that will further enhance learning experiences and facilitate better user engagement. Let's take a quick look at the key highlights of this upcoming release.

 

Custom Pages on the New Learning Home Page

You can create unique custom pages with banners and custom sections tailored to your learners' needs. These custom pages are accessible to learners from the  (activities menu) on their new Learning home page. You can also allow learners to access custom pages through banners and custom cards, which serve as entry points that make it easier to find and use the custom pages.

You can create up to 5 custom pages from System Administration -- Application Administration -- Custom Pages. Within a custom page, you can add banners and create up to 6 sections containing custom cards in each section.

Depending on the active period, the status of cards and banners, and the organizations or user groups to which learners belong, they can view the following content on a custom page:

  • the top 5 banners
  • the top 8 cards in each section

Make sure you have permission to Manage Custom Experience.

How It Looks Now

 

 New Team View for SuccessFactors Learning

If you are using the new Learning home page, you can now use the new Team View page to view and manage learning assignments for your team.

From the new Learning home page, you can open the new Team View page by selecting Team View from the Dashboards menu. The new Team View page opens with your team members listed in the left-hand region.

You can select a team member to see a detailed view of their learning in the right-hand detail view. If you have permission to view team members as an alternate manager or HR business partner a drop-down menu will appear next to the My Team region header and you can select the appropriate role. Team members are sorted alphabetically, with those who have overdue items appearing first. The More Actions menu lets you perform the following actions for your team members:

  • Manage Assignments
  • Add to Learning History
  • Manage Enrollment

You can also use the Team View page to view and act on the individual learning details for each team member.

 

Approvals for Learning Requests in the New Learning Experience

Employees, managers, instructors, and HRBPs with approval roles can now accept or decline pending learning assignment requests sent by learners directly on the new Learning home page.

As approvers, you can either accept or decline the requests (with or without a comment) directly on the cards or follow the View Details link on the card popup to learn more about the requests before responding.

How It Looks Now

The following screen displays an external training request card for approval in the Requests for Me section.

In the previous version, approvers could view and act on their pending approvals only on the Platform home page. These approvals weren't shown on the new Learning home page.

 

The New Learning Home Page Now Supports Delegation Tasks

When managers or supervisors (delegator) assign another user (delegate) to act on their behalf under certain requirements, for example, in their absence, the delegate user can now switch their role temporarily to act as their manager directly on the new Learning home page.

As a delegate, you can go to the  (dashboard menu) and choose Act as to see a list of all managers who have assigned you as their delegate. When you choose a delegator from Act as, you can access their new Learning home page and perform tasks for the manager and their team members, based on the assigned permissions.

For example, if a manager assigns the Approvals permissions to a delegate, the delegate can access the approval cards directly in the Request for Me section of the new Learning home page to act on pending approvals on behalf of the delegator. Delegates can also access the Approvals link from the  (activities menu) that navigates to the Pending Approvals page.

In the delegator's view, an information message appears confirming that you're acting on their behalf across all pages you access.

  • Ensure that the new Learning home page is enabled.
  • Ensure that you've enabled Predefined Permissions and Selected Permissions, based on your requirements, on the Global Application Settings page that allow managers to assign permissions to delegates.
  • Managers have assigned permissions to delegates on the Options and Settings page of the new Learning home page

In the previous version, delegates had to navigate to the legacy Learning experience to act on behalf of their manager as there was no option available from the new Learning home page.

 

Overdue Training Reminder Enhancements

You can now send overdue training reminders to your team from the Home Page, The Learning Home Page, and the Team View Page.

From the Home Page and the Learning Home Page you have the option to review the list of recipients and decide if you want to be copied on each reminder email. The Team View Page allows you to send individual reminders to team members.

Training Reminders on the Home Page

Training Reminders on the Learning Home Page

 

Training Reminders on the Team View Page

SAP has allowed us to send overdue training reminders to provide useful features to our customers.

 

Automatic Import of Open Content Network Items into Learning

You can now automatically import learning content from Open Content Network (OCN) providers to make their courses available to your learners.

This enhancement saves you time by eliminating the need to manually import learning content from OCN providers and ensures a seamless integration of OCN courses into Learning.

Configuration:

 

 

Browse Library Feature for Enhanced Search

Learners can use the Browse Library feature to select a category and refine their library search results. A category appears on the list when it is added to a learning activity that is available in the learner’s library. The category title as it appears in the list is drawn from the category Description in References  Learning Activities  Categories. The category won’t appear on the list if the Description field is left blank. If the learner's library doesn't contain any learning activities with added categories, the list will be empty.

 

Users need the Library  Access -- Advanced Library Search permission in their role.

 

Changes to Permission for Creating Custom Content in Learning Administration

To create and manage custom content, such as banners, custom cards, and custom pages, in the Learning Administration tool, you need the new permission, Manage Custom Experience.

 

 

Previously, the Edit System Configuration permission was required to create and manage custom content. However, the permission is no longer needed.

 

New Learning Experience for Users with an External Shopping Account

We now support the new Learning experience for users with an external shopping account type. This includes enhanced search with the Browse Library feature and an improved user experience for accessing coupons and the shopping cart.

The new Learning experience is now available for users with a Shopping Account Type of External.

From the new Learning home page, users can access learning activities and search the library with the enhanced search capability.

Users need the Library  Access -- Advanced Library Search permission in their role for enhanced search. This is the same permission required to access legacy search in Learning. No additional permission is required if a user has access to legacy search.

 

As we look ahead to the 2H 2024 release of SAP SuccessFactors Learning, it’s exciting to see the dedication to improving the user experience, enhancing administrative capabilities, and promoting flexibility and customization. In my opinion, SAP has made significant progress in enhancing the learning experience, and it's a big step forward to witness the Integrated Learning Experience (ILX) coming together. These updates not only meet the evolving needs of learners and administrators but also mark a significant advancement in integrating learning and development seamlessly into organizational growth. It's thrilling to see how these innovations promise to elevate the learning landscape.

 

Note: All images are taken from the sales demo instance.

entry.title: PACG connector Add on upgrade process in SAP FSM
entry.content:
Purpose:

This blog aims to describe the process for the Service pack Add on upgrade (recommended by Proaxia) in SAP FSM.

Introduction:

What is Proaxia?

Proaxia is the intellectual property owner and developer of the SAP FSM Cloud Connector, as well as an implementation partner of multiple SAP FSM installations with SAP ECC/S4 Integrations from 10 to 1000+ users.

 

What does the Architecture look like?

 

 

(Source: Official Portal of Proaxia for SAP Field service management)

 

How is the Communication set up?

The SAP FSM Cloud connector is a combination of an ABAP Add-on and a Message Broker (that acts as a middleware between SAP S/4 and SAP FSM cloud systems).

For the purpose of communication to and from the Message Broker, Webservices are used.

(In order to have simplicity and conciseness of the content, we would skip the basics of Connector set up and Message broker installation)

 

What is a Service pack?

 

  • A Service pack is basically a configurable package of features released quarterly in a year, by Proaxia. It is usually accompanied by a compatible Message broker version upgrade.
  • The requirement to upgrade a Service pack to the latest version is necessitated to keep a consistency with the current version widely used by the other customers and have a continued support as recommended by Proaxia (as and when a new Service pack version is released).
  • The new features can include the implementation of relevant SAP Notes and/or configuration changes.
  • Additionally, there are some versions that also include new transaction codes (along with the requirements to update the roles and authorizations to access the same) and the previous transaction codes being obsoleted.

 

Steps to update SAP FSM Add on:

 

Prerequisite: Before the start of the add on upgrade, a full system backup is ensured to be completed. This enables the system to be restored from latest backup taken, in the case of any issues.

 

 

  1. Download of the Service pack Add on files (from the Proaxia Portal available here extracted from a ZIP file) by the System Administrator team.
  2. As soon as the SAP FSM Add on component is installed, SPAU and SPDD steps (that enable adding the modifications as a part of the Upgrade in the SAP standard objects) are performed by the technical team.
  3. Login to the Message Broker server and stop the Service.

 

4. Support pack is uploaded in the system:

 

5. Click ‘Ok’:

 

6. Click ‘Ok’:

 

7. Select ‘Background process’ to execute addon upgrade in background mode:

 

8. Click ‘Skip’:

 

9. Click ‘Yes’:

 

10. Click ‘Ok’:

 

11. The Background jobs are displayed:

 

12. Once the job gets executed successfully, perform SPDD adjustment:

 

 

13. Execution message gets displayed:

 

 

14. Click ‘Yes’:

 

 

 

15. Activation message is displayed:

 

 

16. Click ‘Yes’:

 

 

17. Successful execution message is displayed:

 

 

 

18. Add on Upgrade completed successfully with the message logs displayed:

 

 

References:

  1. Set up documentation provided by the Administrator team.
  2. Official website of Proaxia: https://proaxia-prod-doc.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/PFCC/pages/1039434012/About+FSM+Cloud+Connector

entry.title: Read \ Write Data between HANA Datalake and HANA On-Prem DB
entry.content:
A simple guide to Read \ Write table data between SAP HANA Datalake and SAP HANA On-Premises DB. 

Key topics include:

  • Export from HANA On-Prem DB and Import to HANA Datalake Filesystem in CSV and PARQUET Formats using HANA Cloud.
  • Export from HANA On-Prem DB and import to the HANA Datalake Filesystem in CSV and PARQUET formats using HANA Datalake Relational Engine.

 

Export from HANA On-Prem DB and import to the HANA Datalake Filesystem in CSV and PARQUET formats using HANA Datalake Relational Engine

 

1. Creation of HANA On-Prem Remote Server in HANA Datalake Relational Engine

Step 1: Open SQL Console

From the Database Explorer of SAP HANA Datalake Relational Engine, open the SQL Console.

Step 2: Create HANA On-Prem Remote Server

Execute the following SQL query to create the remote server for the HANA On-Prem system

 

CREATE SERVER REMOTE_SERVER CLASS 'HANAODBC' USING
'Driver=libodbcHDB.so;
ConnectTimeout=0;
CommunicationTimeout=15000;
RECONNECT=0;
ServerNode= hanahdb.onprem.sap.server:30241;
ENCRYPT=TRUE;
sslValidateCertificate=False;
UID=USERNAME;
PWD=PaSsWoRd;
UseCloudConnector=ON;
LocationID=SCC-LOC-01';

 

Please note the following

  • REMOTE_SERVER: This is an example name. Replace it with the actual source name
  • hanahdb.onprem.sap.server and 30241: These are the example server name and port. Replace them with the required HANA On-Prem server details
  • USERNAME and PaSsWoRd: Replace these with valid credentials
  • SCC-LOC-01: Replace it with the valid Cloud Connector Location name
Step 3: Verify the Remote Server Connection

Run the following SQL query to check if the newly created remote source is functioning correctly

 

CALL sp_remote_tables('REMOTE_SERVER');

 

If the output lists all the tables of the HANA On-Prem database, the remote server has been created successfully

Step 4: Check the Remote Server Details

To view the details of the newly created remote server, execute the following query:

 

SELECT * FROM SYSSERVER;

 

 

2. Create a Virtual Table in HANA Datalake Relational Engine for HANA On-Prem Table

Create a Existing (Virtual) Table

To create a existing table (virtual table) that points to a table in the HANA On-Prem database, execute the following SQL query

 

CREATE EXISTING TABLE VT_TESTMYTABLE AT 'REMOTE_SERVER..SCHEMA_NAME.TABLE_NAME';

 

Please note the following

  • VT_TESTMYTABLE: This is an example virtual table name. Replace it with the required name
  • REMOTE_SERVER: Replace this with the name of the newly created remote server
  • SCHEMA_NAME: Replace it with the schema name of the table in the HANA On-Prem database
  • TABLE_NAME: Replace this with the actual table name in the HANA On-Prem database

 

3. Export / Import Operations from HANA Datalake Relational Engine to HANA Datalake Filesystem

Export Virtual Table Data
  • Once the virtual table is created in HANA Datalake Relational Engine, you can use SQL commands or tools to export its data
  • For a detailed guide on performing the export and import operations in CSV or PARQUET formats, refer to the blog https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/read-write-data-between-hana-datalake-and-hana-cloud-db/ba-p/13894067

 

Export from HANA On-Prem and Import to HANA Datalake Filesystem in CSV and PARQUET Formats using HANA Cloud

 

1. Creation of HANA On-Prem Remote Source in HANA Cloud

Step 1: Login to the HANA Cloud Database
  • Open Database Explorer of your SAP HANA Cloud Database
  • Login to your HANA Cloud Database Instance and expand the Catalog to locate Remote Sources
Step 2: Add a Remote Source
  • Right-click on Remote Sources and select Add Remote Source
  • Provide the necessary details
    • Source Name: REMOTE_SOURCE_NAME (This is an example, replace it with the appropriate name).
    • Adapter Name: HANA (ODBC).
    • Source Location: indexserver.
Step 3: Adapter Properties Configuration
  • Default driver libodbcHDB.so will be selected automatically
  • Provide:
    • Server: hanahdb.onprem.sap.server (example, replace with your required server).
    • Port: 30241 (example, replace with the correct port number).
Step 4: Extra Adapter Properties
  • Enter the configuration: useHaasSocksProxy=true;sccLocationId=SCC-LOC-01;encrypt=yes;sslValidateCertificate=False

Note: SCC-LOC-01 is an example Cloud Connector name. Replace it with the correct one

Step 5: Credentials Setup
  • Select Technical User as the credentials mode
  • Provide valid Username and Password
Step 6: Save the Remote Source
  • After entering all the details, click Save
  • Alternatively, you can use the SQL query below to create the remote source:

 

CREATE REMOTE SOURCE REMOTE_SOURCE_NAME
ADAPTER "hanaodbc"
CONFIGURATION 'ServerNode=hanahdb.onprem.sap.server:30241;useHaasSocksProxy=true;sccLocationId=SCC-LOC-01;encrypt=yes;sslValidateCertificate=False;'
WITH CREDENTIAL TYPE 'PASSWORD'
USING 'user=Username;password=Password';

 

Step 7: Verify the Remote Source
  • Run the following SQL command to check if the newly created remote source is working

 

CALL PUBLIC.CHECK_REMOTE_SOURCE('REMOTE_SOURCE_NAME');

 

  • If the command executes successfully without errors, the remote source is functional.
Step 8: View the Remote Source
  • Expand the Catalog of the HANA Cloud Database Instance
  • Right-click on Remote Sources and select Show Remote Sources to confirm your connection

 

2. Create a Virtual Table in HANA Cloud for HANA On-Prem Table

Step 9: Open Remote Source
  • Right-click on the newly created Remote Source (REMOTE_SOURCE_NAME) and select Open
Step 10: Search for On-Prem Table (Remote Objects)
  • Use the Schema and Object filters to search for the required On-Prem table
  • Click Search to display the list of available remote objects (tables)
Step 11: Create Virtual Object
  • Select the desired table from the list
  • Click on Create Virtual Object(s)
Step 12: Define Virtual Table Details
  • Provide a name for the virtual table
  • Select the target schema in your HANA Cloud Database
  • Click Create to finish the process

The newly created virtual table in HANA Cloud can now be used for operations, including exporting data to the HANA Datalake Filesystem.

 

3. Export / Import Operations from HANA Cloud to HANA Datalake Filesystem

Export Virtual Table Data
  • Once the virtual table is created in HANA Datalake Relational Engine, you can use SQL commands or tools to export its data
  • For a detailed guide on performing the export and import operations in CSV or PARQUET formats, refer to the blog https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/read-write-data-between-hana-datalake-and-hana-cloud-db/ba-p/13894067

entry.title: Comprendre les Firefighter IDs dans SAP
entry.content:
Dans le cadre de la gestion des accès d’urgence dans SAP, le processus de FireFighter IDs (ou Emergency Access Management, EAM) joue un rôle crucial en matière de sécurité et de gestion des risques. Ce processus permet aux utilisateurs de disposer d’un accès temporaire à des comptes avec plus d’autorisations, tout en assurant un suivi rigoureux de leurs actions.

Qu’est-ce qu’un Firefighter ID dans SAP ?

Un Firefighter ID est un identifiant qui offre à un utilisateur un accès temporaire à des autorisations et des comptes avec des droits et autorisations supérieures, souvent nécessaires pour répondre à des situations d'urgence ou critiques pour l’entreprise. Cet accès est distinct des permissions quotidiennes de l’utilisateur et permet de séparer les accès sensibles des autorisations régulières. Ce rôle de FireFighter s’accompagne de mécanismes de suivi détaillés pour garantir une traçabilité complète des actions effectuées.

Pourquoi le processus EAM est-il nécessaire ?

Le processus EAM consiste à octroyer des accès temporaires à des utilisateurs spécifiques afin qu’ils puissent accomplir des tâches qui sortent de leur champ d’autorisation habituel. Cela est particulièrement utile en cas de problèmes urgents où une intervention rapide est requise. Chaque action effectuée sous un Firefighter ID est méticuleusement enregistrée pour permettre un audit ultérieur, garantissant ainsi la transparence et la sécurité des opérations.

Défis liés à la mise en œuvre d’un processus EAM

  1. Définir correctement les comptes d’utilisateur et de FireFighter : L’un des principaux défis est de bien définir la distinction entre les comptes d’utilisateur classiques et les FireFighter IDs. Il est essentiel de trouver un équilibre entre les fonctions quotidiennes de l'utilisateur et l'utilisation d’un FireFighter ID, réservé aux situations d’urgence. Une mauvaise gestion de cet équilibre peut entraîner une utilisation abusive de l’ID FireFighter pour des tâches routinières, ce qui surcharge les employés responsables des audits.
  2. Retards dans les audits des journaux : Un autre défi est d’assurer des audits rapides après l'utilisation d'un FireFighter ID. Si les audits ne sont pas réalisés à temps, des risques potentiels comme la fraude ou des vulnérabilités de sécurité peuvent persister sans être détectés. Le manque de documentation sur les raisons d’accès d’urgence complique également l’audit, rendant la tâche des administrateurs plus difficile.
  1. Difficulté à interpréter les journaux EAM : Les journaux EAM dans SAP GRC peuvent être complexes à analyser pour les utilisateurs métiers non techniques. Cela peut conduire à des validations inappropriées, où les actions effectuées sous le FireFighter ID ne sont pas entièrement comprises avant l'approbation des audits.

Méthodologies FireFighter

Il existe deux principales méthodologies pour l’utilisation des FireFighter IDs dans SAP :

  • Méthodologie par utilisateur : L'utilisateur dispose d’un identifiant distinct pour ses tâches quotidiennes et d’un FireFighter ID pour les activités d’urgence.
  • Méthodologie par rôle : L’utilisateur garde le même identifiant pour toutes ses tâches, mais un rôle spécifique de FireFighter lui est attribué en cas d’urgence.

Définir l’accès pour le processus EAM

Deux options sont généralement considérées pour la gestion des accès :

  • Accès pré-approuvé : L’utilisateur dispose d'une autorisation préalable pour accéder au FireFighter ID. Cela permet une réponse rapide, mais présente des risques de sécurité plus élevés.
  • Accès avec approbation supplémentaire : L’utilisateur doit demander une autorisation avant d'utiliser le FireFighter ID. Cette méthode est plus sécurisée mais peut entraîner des retards.

entry.title: Re: Talent Intelligence Hub - Myths and Misconceptions - Part 2 (Migration)
entry.content:
@cstal you're very welcome!

  • Yes, you can use current GP without OMP. Only if you want the Latest Career Worksheet experience you will need OMP, TIH and CWS in place.
  • Currently, there is no option to differentiate permissions by attribute type. So if a user has the permission to add attributes to their GP, the can do that for all attributes that exist in the customer's library, regardless of attribute type. I believe product management is looking into more granular permissions, so please check the road map explorer and support any potential enhancement request related to this.
  • Competencies and skills are already integrated across the entire suite when you enable TIH. So you can use skills in Performance Management, Succession, OMP, LMS and so on. Only LMS and OMP support custom attribute types on top of that at the moment.

Good luck with the upgrade! 🙂

entry.title: I need Badi of Purchase Order change
From BEx to AfO to SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Several years ago, I published a post concerning The BEx Broadcasting Need and SAP Analytics Cloud. It is time to update the use cases from that post.

BEx broadcasting has been an important tool for organizations using BW. It has effectively served important communication workflows over the past twenty-plus years, allowing organizations to distribute reports to a wide variety of users, partners and customers. However, with the need to push content out to stakeholders continuing to be a critical requirement for many of these organizations, and with this capability only available in older versions of SAP BW, such organizations need to deploy an alternative to BEx broadcasting as part of their move to newer technologies like BW/4HANA, S/4HANA, and Datasphere.

While there are still a considerable number of customers continuing to use BEx Analyzer and Broadcaster, a majority of customers have moved on to Analysis for Office. As organizations undertake digital transformations and adopt SAP’s cloud-based offerings, including SAP Analytics Cloud, the need to move beyond BEx becomes more and more pressing.

In her recent update on Analysis for Office, SAP’s Jeanne Bigonnet emphasized SAP’s continuing investment in the cloud-based SAP Analytics Cloud Add-In for Microsoft Excel, but she also noted that the desktop edition of Analysis for Office has a user base of around 1 million, and SAP will continue to support this user base.

From BEx Analyzer to Analysis for Office

In my original post, I contemplated the possibility of implementing SAP Analytics Cloud stories to fulfill the business process workflows that were previously using BEx Analyzer. While that continues to be an effective approach which many will fully or partially employ, it does require the creation of a set of stories and the development of new skillsets by BEx report developers. The preferred path for the majority of organizations currently using BEx is to migrate their BEx reports to Analysis for Office.

But getting the reports moved to Analysis for Office is only half of the journey to replacing the BEx Broadcaster workflows. The next step is to address report scheduling, broadcasting and distribution requirements, which present new challenges.

SAP BusinessObjects provides scheduling and broadcasting capabilities to Analysis for Office users, but the digital transformations of SAP customers are taking these organizations beyond SAP BusinessObjects and into the cloud-based analytics of SAP Analytics Cloud. The move to SAP Analytics Cloud is not cosmetic: it is a shift to advanced analytics, visualization, planning, and integration with SAP’s cloud-based enterprise offerings, creating a unified data fabric. These organizations will face growing pressure to move their BI workflows to take advantage of new capabilities.

From SAP BusinessObjects to SAP Analytics Cloud

As SAP Analytics Cloud adoption continues, organizations are looking for ways to move their Analysis for Office reports to the newer cloud-based analytics so they can remove another use case from SAP BusinessObjects as they prepare to retire the venerable BI platform. But moving Analysis for Office reports to the SAP Analytics Cloud Repository creates a new problem: SAP Analytics Cloud does not support Analysis for Office scheduling and broadcasting.

To move beyond SAP BusinessObjects, organizations using Analysis for Office need to establish scheduling and broadcasting in SAP Analytics Cloud.

With the need to push content out to stakeholders continuing to be a critical requirement for many of these organizations, and with the sunsetting of BEx reports, such organizations need to deploy an alternative to BEx broadcasting as part of their digital transformation and move to S/4HANA, BW/4HANA, Datasphere, and other updated technologies. Analysis for Office meets these needs, but SAP Analytics Cloud lacks support for AfO scheduling and broadcasting. This lack is both a barrier to SAP Analytics Cloud adoption and a barrier to SAP BusinessObjects retirement.

Centralizing Analysis for Office reports in the SAP Analytics Cloud Repository is a first step toward making Analysis for Office an integral part of your data fabric, with all the benefits such integration entails, including improvements to data governance and security.

Scheduling & Broadcasting Analysis for Office Reports in SAP Analytics Cloud

Organizations can overcome the lack of Analysis for Office report scheduling and broadcasting in SAP Analytics for Cloud by deploying APOS Publisher for Cloud.

APOS Publisher for Cloud (available on the SAP Store) smooths the adoption of both Analysis for Office and SAP Analytics Cloud by complementing Analysis for Office workflows with scheduling & broadcasting capabilities. It creates a centralized and unified scheduling and broadcasting environment for SAP Analytics Cloud reports, and SAP Analysis for Office reports.

The solution also includes a web user interface that provides a simplified means of meeting all of the scheduling needs of most Analysis for Office users.

APOS Publisher for Cloud Web User Interface

 

In summary, the APOS Publisher for Cloud solution helps organizations to:

  • Move SAP Analysis for Office to SAP Analytics Cloud with scheduling and publishing capabilities
  • Support SAP Analytics Cloud adoption
  • Centralize scheduling and broadcasting for better data governance and security
  • Simplify scheduling with a web user interface
  • Replace BEx Broadcaster workflows

entry.title: Extract ANLA Asset Master Record Segment Data in CSV File Using ABAP Program For Selected Cost Cente
entry.content:
FIXED ASSET - Extract ANLA Asset Master Record Segment Data in CSV File Using ABAP Program For Selected Cost Center

ABAP Program - Z_ANLA_FIXED_ASSET 

  • Program Type – ABAP  
  • ABAP Program Name – Z_ANLA_FIXED_ASSET 
  • Tcode - Z_ANLA_FA 
  • Entity Description - Asset Master Record Segment 
  • Output File Location - “C:\tmp” 
  • Output File Name- ANLA.CSV 
  • Output File Type – CSV (Column with Semicolon separator (;) 
  • SAP Table Name – ANLA , ANLB 
  • List of fields to be extracted -  
  • ANLN1 
  • ANLKL 
  • TXT50 
  • AKTIV 

-------- Program Start From Here-------------------------

REPORT  Z_ANLA_FIXED_ASSET.
 
TYPE-POOLS: VRM.
 
DATA: NAME  TYPE VRM_ID,
      LIST  TYPE VRM_VALUES,
      VALUE LIKE LINE OF LIST.
 
 
PARAMETERS: ENTITY(10) AS LISTBOX VISIBLE LENGTH 10.
PARAMETERS : OPATH TYPE STRING DEFAULT '\\SERVERNAME\tmp\ANLA.csv'.
 
AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.
 
NAME = 'ENTITY'.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK01'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK01'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK75'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK75'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK21'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK21'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK31'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK31'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK41'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK41'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK02'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK02'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK73'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK73'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK04'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK04'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
VALUE-KEY = 'UK61'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK61'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.
 
CALL FUNCTION 'VRM_SET_VALUES' EXPORTING ID = NAME VALUES = LIST.
 
START-OF-SELECTION.
type-pools:TRUXS.
data: begin of itab occurs 0,
      anln1 like anla-anln1,
  anlkl like anla-anlkl,
  txt50 like anla-txt50,
  aktiv like anla-aktiv,
  BUKRS like ANLA-BUKRS,
      end of itab.
 
data:  itab1 type TRUXS_T_TEXT_DATA.
 select  ANLA~anln1
ANLA~anlkl
ANLA~txt50
ANLA~aktiv
   ANLA~BUKRS
 
         from ANLA
         INNER JOIN ANLB ON ANLB~ANLN1 = ANLA~ANLN1
         into table itab
WHERE ANLA~BUKRS = ENTITY AND ANLA~DEAKT IN ('00000000').
 
CALL FUNCTION 'SAP_CONVERT_TO_CSV_FORMAT'
 EXPORTING
   I_FIELD_SEPERATOR          = ','
  TABLES
    I_TAB_SAP_DATA             = itab
 CHANGING
   I_TAB_CONVERTED_DATA       =  itab1
 EXCEPTIONS
   CONVERSION_FAILED          = 1
   OTHERS                     = 2
          .
IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
 MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
         WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
ENDIF.
 
   CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
    EXPORTING
      filename = OPATH
    TABLES
      data_tab = itab1
    EXCEPTIONS
      OTHERS   = 1.

entry.title: IBM Granite.13b.chat Large Language Model- Now GA on the SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
SAP’s generative AI Hub is the central place for BTP application developers to access and use generative AI tools and models, and developers can now choose IBM Granite as their large language model (LLM). This article will explain some of Granite’s unique characteristics and then show you how to access and use it.

IBM Granite.13b.chat is a decoder, English-only, enterprise-grade LLM and part of a series of IBM foundation models for generative artificial intelligence tasks. IBM stands behind Granite LLMs with IP indemnification protection according to its Standard Terms and Conditions, to provide users with peace of mind. Granite.13b.chat was trained on enterprise-relevant content that meets rigorous data governance, regulatory, and risk criteria defined and enforced by the IBM AI Ethics Code and Chief Privacy Office. You can read the details here: https://www.ibm.com/downloads/cas/X9W4O6BM. Granite.13b.chat delivers high accuracy for retrieval augmented generation (RAG), summarization, content generation, named entity recognition, insight extraction, and classification.

A considerable portion of Granite training data is from business domains. For business-specific tasks in domains such as finance, some IBM performance benchmarks suggest that the accuracy of Granite models is on par with larger models (70B parameter+) at lower latency and, due to its smaller size (13B parameter), lower infrastructure spending.

AI capabilities in SAP AI Core & SAP AI Launchpad that utilize the generative AI hub can leverage Granite.13b.chat.

Get started by:

  1. Learning how to use foundational models on SAP AI Core (https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-consumption-llm.html), and
  2. learning how to prompt Granite.13b.chat (https://dataplatform.cloud.ibm.com/docs/content/wsj/analyze-data/fm-models-ibm-chat.html?context=wx&audience=wdp).

For more information about Granite.13b.chat and instruction-tuning details, see the model card (https://dataplatform.cloud.ibm.com/login?context=wx&sign_in_target=%2Fwx%2Fsamples%2Fmodels%2Fibm%2Fgranite-13b-chat-v2%3Fcontext%3Dwx%3Fcontext%3Dwx%26audience%3Dwdp).
